title: tetradymia squamata
intro: Tetradymia squamata, also known as rockrose or granite rockrose, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Tetradymia squamata include its potential to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of Tetradymia squamata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
